name: @cloakcode/extension
description: VS Code extension host: vscode.lm model access, transcript observer, and the localhost bridge server.

cloakcode.installHook.markdownDescription: Automatically install the CloakCode Copilot notifier hook (`~/.copilot/hooks/cloakcode.json`) on activation. The hook is a non-intrusive notifier (it never approves or denies tool calls); it only powers the live-pending overlay. This is a **single per-environment file shared by every VS Code window**, so the setting is machine-scoped — set it in User or Remote settings, not per-workspace (a per-workspace toggle would be meaningless: the global file already fires for every window). Turn it off to manage the hook yourself; disabling does not delete an already-installed file.

cloakcode.surfaceDebounceMs.markdownDescription: Milliseconds to wait before surfacing a pending tool call to the CloakCode client. VS Code often auto-approves or auto-answers a call within this window; debouncing lets those resolve first so they never flicker a card on the phone. Raise it if fast auto-approved calls still flash a card; set it to `0` to surface immediately.

cloakcode.publicUrl.markdownDescription: Public URL of your tunnel to the CloakCode gateway, shown by **CloakCode: Show Phone Link** and its QR — e.g. a VS Code **Public** forwarded port or a Dev Tunnel `https://<id>-<port>.<region>.devtunnels.ms`. Leave empty to use VS Code's automatic forwarding (`asExternalUri`): that is phone-reachable in Codespaces/remotes, but returns a **desktop-loopback** URL in local dev containers — set this then.

cloakcode.tunnel.markdownDescription: How **CloakCode: Show Phone Link** gets a phone-reachable URL when `cloakcode.publicUrl` is empty. `off`: use VS Code's automatic forwarding (`asExternalUri`). `devtunnel`: auto-host the gateway on a **private** Microsoft Dev Tunnel and parse its public URL for you — no manual tunnel or URL paste. Requires the `devtunnel` CLI installed and `devtunnel user login`. The tunnel is **private** (sign-in required to open the link), never anonymous.

cloakcode.port.markdownDescription: Localhost port for the CloakCode gateway (bound to `127.0.0.1` only). `0` (default) picks a free **ephemeral** port each launch — avoids collisions when several windows/instances run. Set a fixed port (e.g. `7801`) to **lock** it, which also keeps the phone/tunnel URL stable across reloads. (The dev launch overrides this with `CLOAKCODE_PORT`.)

cloakcode.instanceId.markdownDescription: A **human-readable label** for this environment + workspace — shown in the client and used as the Dev Tunnel name. Display only: sessions route by `sessionId` and group by workspace, so it is **never** used for routing or identity. Empty = auto: `<local|wsl|devcontainer|ssh|codespaces|tunnel>:<workspace-folder>` (using the dev container's `name` when available). **Set this only in Workspace settings — never User/Remote** (a value there would mislabel every other workspace). VS Code has no workspace-only scope, so those tabs still appear; leave them blank.

cloakcode.gatewayUrl.markdownDescription: WebSocket URL of a standalone **CloakCode gateway** to connect to as a provider (e.g. `ws://localhost:7900`). When set and reachable, this window does **not** host its own bridge or serve the PWA — it connects OUT to the gateway and speaks only the protocol (the gateway serves the app + owns the phone link). Empty (default) = **embedded** mode: the extension serves its own PWA + bridge. If the gateway is unreachable at activation, it falls back to embedded. See docs/03 ‘Explicit gateway’. The `CLOAKCODE_GATEWAY_URL` env var overrides this setting (the dev-container F5 flow uses it to point at the host gateway).

scripts.package: pnpm bundle && pnpm build:web && node scripts/package-vsix.mjs
